title: Cross-Side Negative Externalities

content.statement: One side of a platform can harm the other.
content.question: Could too many members overwhelm your supply side? Could too much supply dilute the member experience?
content.equation: CSNE = Σ(Benefit to One Side - Harm to Other)
sop: Identify the two sides of your platform or business model that depend on each other (buyers or sellers, members or supply, users or creators).
List 2-3 scenarios where growth on one side could plausibly hurt the other.
Estimate the Benefit to the growing side and the Harm to the other side for each scenario (see calculation, page 6).
Subtract Harm from Benefit for each scenario and sum them to see your net effect (see calculation, page 6).
Set an early-warning metric (wait times, satisfaction scores, complaint rate) on the side most at risk and monitor it monthly.
content.explainer: Every two-sided market has a tipping point where growth on one side hurts the other. Know yours.
questions: Could too many members overwhelm your supply side? Could too much supply dilute the member experience?
Is there a growth metric you're currently celebrating that might actually be degrading the experience for the other side of your business?
If you 10x'd one side of your marketplace overnight, which specific complaint would you expect from the other side first?

definitions.def: When more users on side A make the platform more valuable for side B.

content.equation_example: Benefit to demand side from more supply rated 8 out of 10, Harm to supply side from more competition rated 6 out of 10. 8 minus 6 equals plus 2 net. Positive and rising means growth on one side is still net-helping the system. Negative means you've crossed the tipping point where growth on one side is actively hurting the other.
content.closing_statement: Growth on one side can quietly cost you the other.

caption:
Principle Nº 79: Cross-Side Negative Externalities

One side of a platform can harm the other. Every two-sided market has a tipping point where growth on one side hurts the other.

Ask yourself: Could too many members overwhelm your supply side? Could too much supply dilute the member experience?
